content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 5, 1987

Gregory G. Cowart, Chief of Police
City of Gilroy
7370 Rosanna Street
Gilroy, CA 95020

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. I-87-249

Dear Mr. Cowart:

You have requested information concerning conflicts of interest
for appointed city employees who receive gratuities. We have no
sample policies which specifically address that subject. However,
the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act1/
require that all public officials with decision-making authority
comply with disclosure and disqualification requirements regarding
gratuities and other gifts.

Specifically, public officials at all levels of state and local
government are required to (1) disclose gifts totaling $50 or more
from a single source in a calendar year and (2) disqualify from
participating in any decision which will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from the
effect on the public generally, on the donor or intermediary of
gifts totaling $250 or more made or promised to them within the
preceding 12 months. (Sections 87103, 87207 and 87302.) The
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act are
explained in greater detail in the enclosed pamphlet entitled "A
Guide to the Political Reform Act of 1974 -- California's Conflict
of Interest Law for Public Officials." Also enclosed is a copy of
the Political Reform Act.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[illegible]
By: Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:jaj
Enclosures

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: You have requested information concerning conflicts of interest for appointed city employees who receive gratuities.
sections.conclusion: Public officials at all levels of state and local government are required to (1) disclose gifts totaling $50 or more from a single source in a calendar year and (2) disqualify from participating in any decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the donor or intermediary of gifts totaling $250 or more made or promised to them within the preceding 12 months.
sections.question_synthetic: What conflicts of interest provisions apply to appointed city employees who receive gratuities?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act requires all public officials with decision-making authority to disclose gifts of $50 or more annually from a single source and to disqualify themselves from decisions affecting donors of gifts totaling $250 or more in the preceding 12 months. The FPPC provided detailed guidance materials and referenced relevant Government Code sections 87103, 87207, and 87302.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion extracted from letter body. Document is FPPC response signed by Counsel. File number I-87-249 indicates incoming request file.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested information concerning conflicts of interest for appointed city employees who receive gratuities.

CONCLUSION: Public officials at all levels of state and local government are required to (1) disclose gifts totaling $50 or more from a single source in a calendar year and (2) disqualify from participating in any decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the donor or intermediary of gifts totaling $250 or more made or promised to them within the preceding 12 months.

However, the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act1/ require that all public officials with decision-making authority comply with disclosure and disqualification requirements regarding gratuities and other gifts. embedding.summary: FPPC responds to City of Gilroy Police Chief's inquiry about conflict of interest policies for appointed employees receiving gratuities, explaining disclosure and disqualification requirements under the Political Reform Act.
